articles.context: The warning from the ACSO comes amid heightened scrutiny of civil society organizations in Ethiopia, particularly following the 2021 Tigray conflict, which exacerbated tensions between the government and NGOs. This latest directive reflects ongoing efforts to regulate foreign aid amidst a fragile political landscape.

This event also reflects the broader ambition of East African leaders to stabilize and grow their economies through cooperative housing and trade policies.

articles.title: Kenyan leader sparks uproar after mocking Nigerians' spoken English

articles.context: William Ruto's comments come amid ongoing tensions between Kenya and Nigeria, particularly in the context of regional leadership within the African Union and the Economic Community of West African States (ECOWAS). The rivalry has been heightened since Ruto's election in September 2022, as both nations vie for influence in East and West Africa. This incident also reflects broader issues of linguistic pride and cultural identity within the diverse linguistic landscapes of Africa.

The new guidelines aim to address these issues amid ongoing societal tensions exacerbated by economic challenges and the COVID-19 pandemic, making mediation a critical tool for conflict resolution.

articles.title: Trump set expand refugee program for white South Africans

articles.context: In Kenya, the 2010 Constitution emphasizes the right to dignity and family consent in burial practices, reflecting a broader push for human rights. articles.context: تعيش السودان حالة من الفوضى منذ اندلاع النزاع المسلح في أبريل 2023 بين الجيش السوداني وقوات الدعم السريع، مما أدى إلى تدهور الأوضاع الإنسانية وزيادة الانتهاكات ضد المدنيين. على الرغم من محاولات المجتمع الدولي، بما في ذلك اتفاقية جدة في يونيو 2023، لوقف القتال، فإن الانقسامات داخل القوى المدنية تعكس تعقيدات الصراع وتحديات تحقيق السلام والاستقرار في البلاد.

articles.title: Parliament to review peace deal amendments in Friday sitting
articles.summary: South Sudan’s Transitional National Legislature is set to convene a House Business Committee sitting on Friday, with key agenda items including proposed amendments to the peace agreement and presentation of the Speaker’s 100-day work plan. articles.context: The proposed amendments to South Sudan's peace agreement, originally signed in September 2018, aim to address ongoing tensions and power-sharing disputes among key factions, including President Salva Kiir's government and opposition leader Riek Machar. The upcoming review comes amid heightened concerns over violence and instability, particularly following clashes in Unity State in late 2022, which threaten the fragile peace process and the implementation of the Revitalized Agreement on the Resolution of the Conflict in South Sudan (R-ARCSS).

articles.title: مساعٍ للبرهان لتجاوز التوتر مع الرياض بسبب مواقفه من العدوان الإيراني على الخليج

articles.context: تأتي زيارة الفريق أول ركن عبدالفتاح البرهان إلى السعودية وعمان في ظل توترات متزايدة بين السودان والمملكة بسبب مواقف البرهان من العدوان الإيراني على الخليج، خاصة بعد تصاعد النزاع في المنطقة منذ عام 2019. كما أن هذه الزيارة تتزامن مع جهود البرهان لتعزيز العلاقات الاقتصادية والسياسية مع دول الخليج، في ظل التحديات الاقتصادية التي تواجه السودان بعد الانقلاب العسكري في أكتوبر 2021.

articles.title: Labor ministry revokes 2010 directive, orders direct NSIF remittances
articles.summary: South Sudan’s Ministry of Labour has cancelled a long-standing directive on social insurance contributions and ordered all employers to remit payments directly to the National Social Insurance Fund (NSIF), effective immediately. articles.context: The revocation of Circular No. 03-2010 comes amid ongoing economic challenges in South Sudan, exacerbated by the civil conflict that erupted in 2013 and has led to widespread poverty and unemployment. The National Social Insurance Fund, established to provide social security benefits, has struggled with funding due to inconsistent remittances from employers. This directive aims to strengthen financial accountability and support the country’s recovery efforts following the 2018 Revitalized Peace Agreement.

articles.context: تتجلى الأزمة السودانية الحالية في الصراع بين الجيش السوداني وقوات الدعم السريع، الذي تصاعد منذ اندلاع القتال في أبريل 2023. يأتي هذا الصراع في سياق تاريخي معقد، حيث شهد السودان تحولات سياسية بعد الإطاحة بنظام عمر البشير في 2019، مما أدى إلى تباين في القوى السياسية والعسكرية. تثير المعادلة الأخلاقية بين الطرفين تساؤلات حول شرعية كل منهما، خاصة في ظل غياب اتفاقيات واضحة لإنهاء النزاع.

articles.title: Supreme court adjourns Ajing detention case to April 30

The Supreme Court's decision to adjourn until April 30 reflects the judiciary's role in navigating these complex political dynamics.

articles.context: تأتي هذه التفاهمات الأمنية في سياق تصاعد التوترات في شرق السودان، حيث شهدت المنطقة صراعات مستمرة بين الحكومة المركزية والحركات المسلحة. تمثل "التحالف الفيدرالي لقوى شرق السودان"، الذي يضم مؤتمر البجا وحركة تحرير شرق السودان، جزءاً من الجهود الرامية إلى تحقيق الاستقرار بعد اتفاق جوبا للسلام في 2020. هذه التفاهمات، التي تم توقيعها مؤخراً، تعكس محاولات جديدة لتعزيز الأمن والتنمية في منطقة تعاني من التهميش.

articles.title: Six ways your smartwatch is lying to you, according to science
articles.summary: Your smartwatch is not as accurate as you might think.
articles.context: In Kenya, the growing adoption of wearable technology, including smartwatches, is part of a broader trend towards digital health solutions amid increasing health challenges. However, the accuracy of these devices is critical, especially as the country grapples with issues like non-communicable diseases and a strained healthcare system. As the Kenyan government pushes for improved health data management through initiatives like the 2019 Digital Health Strategy, understanding the limitations of these technologies is essential for effective health interventions.

articles.title: Mashemeji derby in numbers
articles.summary: The two bitter rivals are locked in a tight Premier League title race with six rounds of...
articles.context: The Mashemeji derby, featuring AFC Leopards and Gor Mahia, is not just a football match but a reflection of Kenya's deep-seated rivalries and social dynamics. With both teams vying for the Premier League title, the stakes are heightened amid ongoing tensions in Kenyan football governance, particularly following the 2022 Sports Act that aimed to reform management but has faced resistance. This derby could significantly impact fan morale and club finances as the season approaches its climax.

articles.title: 2027 race: Luo Nyanza and Mt Kenya undergo political reset
articles.summary: The regions have apparently switched 2022 political positions.
articles.context: The Luo Nyanza region, traditionally aligned with opposition figures like Raila Odinga, has seen a shift as President William Ruto's administration seeks to consolidate power in the lead-up to the 2027 elections. Meanwhile, the Mt Kenya region, historically supportive of Ruto, is experiencing internal divisions following the fallout from the 2022 elections, where the Kenya Kwanza coalition faced challenges in maintaining unity. This political reset reflects broader tensions and realignments in Kenya's electoral landscape.

articles.title: ‘Ok oyot’: Words of man missing in Russia-Ukraine war haunt family
articles.summary: By the time the family realised their son had travelled to join a foreign military, it was too...
articles.context: The ongoing Russia-Ukraine war, which escalated in February 2022, has drawn foreign fighters from various countries, including Kenya. The Kenyan government has expressed concern over its citizens joining foreign conflicts, particularly after the 2019 Nairobi attack linked to Al-Shabaab. This situation highlights the complex dynamics of youth radicalization and the allure of foreign military involvement, raising questions about national security and the responsibilities of families and governments in addressing these issues.

articles.title: Focus on non-tariff barriers on new dip in Kenya exports to Uganda, Tanzania
articles.summary: Disputes and rising costs continue to disrupt regional trade flows.
articles.context: Kenya's exports to Uganda and Tanzania have faced significant challenges due to non-tariff barriers, exacerbated by the East African Community (EAC) integration efforts initiated in 2000. Tensions have escalated following the 2021 trade dispute over maize imports, which highlighted regulatory inconsistencies. Additionally, the recent rise in transport costs due to fuel price hikes and the ongoing impacts of the COVID-19 pandemic have further strained trade relations among these neighboring countries.

articles.title: Pain of 26 patients detained at JM Memorial hospital over bills
articles.summary: Most of the patients are detained because they cannot afford to pay the SHA monthly fees.
articles.context: The detention of patients at JM Memorial Hospital highlights the ongoing healthcare crisis in Kenya, exacerbated by the COVID-19 pandemic and economic instability. The government’s failure to implement the Universal Health Coverage (UHC) initiative, launched in 2018, has left many unable to afford medical care, leading to increased reliance on the Social Health Insurance (SHA) scheme. This situation underscores the urgent need for reforms in Kenya's healthcare financing and access to justice for vulnerable populations.

articles.title: Summit or conference: Here is how to know
articles.summary: Meetings, conferences or summits may all concern resolving or managing conflicts.
articles.context: The recent tensions between Kenya and Somalia over maritime boundaries have reignited diplomatic discussions, particularly following the International Court of Justice's ruling in October 2021 favoring Somalia. As both nations seek to stabilize their relationship, the upcoming regional summit in Nairobi on November 15, 2023, aims to address not only maritime disputes but also broader security concerns related to the resurgence of Al-Shabaab. This context underscores the importance of distinguishing between summits and conferences in diplomatic efforts.

articles.title: Uniform cartel row: MPs consider scrapping school uniforms
articles.summary: School uniforms serve as a unique identifiers and marks of school tradition and history.
articles.context: In Kenya, the debate over school uniforms has intensified following allegations of a cartel profiting from uniform sales, highlighted by a parliamentary inquiry in October 2023. This controversy emerges amidst broader discussions on educational reforms and accessibility, particularly in light of the 2018 Competency-Based Curriculum, which aims to enhance learning outcomes. The potential scrapping of uniforms could significantly impact school identity and tradition, as well as the financial burdens on families.

articles.title: How Kenyan girls are shaping the future of ICT
articles.summary: Despite limited resources, young innovators are building solutions.
articles.context: In recent years, Kenya has emerged as a leader in technology and innovation in Africa, particularly following the launch of the National ICT Policy in 2016, which aimed to enhance digital skills among youth. The government's support for initiatives like the Ajira Digital Program, launched in 2017, has empowered young women to pursue careers in ICT, addressing gender disparities in a sector historically dominated by men. This context highlights the critical role of Kenyan girls in shaping the future of technology.

articles.title: ترامب يعلن تمديد وقف إطلاق النار بين إسرائيل ولبنان لمدة ثلاثة أسابيع، ويستبعد استخدام سلاح نووي ضد إيران

articles.context: تأتي هذه الخطوة في سياق تصاعد التوترات بين إسرائيل وحزب الله، خاصة بعد الاشتباكات الحدودية في مايو 2023. كما أن تمديد وقف إطلاق النار يعكس جهود إدارة ترامب للتوسط في السلام بعد اتفاقيات أبراهام التي أُبرمت في عام 2020، والتي شملت تطبيع العلاقات بين إسرائيل ودول عربية. الوضع في لبنان، الذي يعاني من أزمة اقتصادية وسياسية، يزيد من تعقيد هذه الديناميات الإقليمية.

articles.title: محمد الخبيري: ياسمين عبدالعزيز مجتهدة ومن الصعب على أي فنان يحقق نجاح سنتين ورا بعض زيها

articles.context: تأتي تصريحات المخرج محمد الخبيري في وقت يشهد فيه السودان تحديات سياسية واقتصادية كبيرة، خاصة بعد اندلاع النزاع المسلح في أبريل 2023 بين الجيش وقوات الدعم السريع. في ظل هذه الأوضاع، يعتبر نجاح الأعمال الفنية مثل مسلسل "وننسى اللي كان" مصدرًا للأمل والتفاؤل، حيث تسهم في تعزيز الروح المعنوية وتوحيد المجتمع السوداني في مواجهة الأزمات.

articles.title: آيتن عامر: ‏الأخطر من الجهل الثقة في معلومات مغلوطة
articles.summary: أثارت الفنانة آيتن عامر تفاعلًا واسعًا بعد منشور لها عبر منصة “إكس”، علّقت فيه على حالة الجدل المنتشرة عبر مواقع التواصل الاجتماعي. وكتبت آيتن عامر: “‏مش كل جدل يبقى وعي و مش كل اختلاف يبقى فكر عميق خصوصا في زمن الترند”. واختتمت: “‏أخطر حاجة مش الجهل ‏لكن “الثقة في معلومات غلط” ‏دة الكارثة الحقيقية” . …
articles.context: تأتي تصريحات آيتن عامر في وقت تشهد فيه السودان حالة من الفوضى السياسية والاجتماعية بعد اندلاع النزاع المسلح في أبريل 2023 بين الجيش وقوات الدعم السريع. هذا الصراع أدى إلى تفشي المعلومات المغلوطة عبر وسائل التواصل الاجتماعي، مما يزيد من حالة الارتباك والجدل بين المواطنين. في ظل هذه الظروف، تبرز أهمية الوعي الإعلامي والتمييز بين الحقائق والشائعات.

articles.title: Financing Kenya’s industrialisation
articles.summary: Structured capital therefore provides the bridge between policy vision and industrial delivery.
articles.context: Kenya's Vision 2030 aims to transform the country into a newly industrializing, middle-income nation, but financing remains a critical barrier. The ongoing challenges posed by the COVID-19 pandemic and rising global inflation have strained public finances, complicating efforts to attract foreign investment. Additionally, the recent signing of the African Continental Free Trade Area (AfCFTA) agreement in 2021 presents both opportunities and challenges for Kenya's industrial sector, necessitating innovative financing solutions to enhance competitiveness.

articles.title: Champagne for the few or drinking water for all?
articles.summary: The benefit of corruption is private, while the cost is paid by a generation.
articles.context: In Kenya, the ongoing struggle against corruption has intensified since the 2010 Constitution aimed to decentralize power and enhance accountability. High-profile scandals, such as the 2020 COVID-19 funds misappropriation involving government officials, have sparked public outrage. As the country grapples with a severe water crisis, highlighted by the 2021 drought affecting millions, the stark contrast between elite indulgence and widespread deprivation underscores the urgent need for systemic reform and equitable resource distribution.

articles.title: Graft, dictatorship and ‘goonism’
articles.summary: Everyone is a crook: if you give your cousin or some relative an assignment, a Probox starts...
articles.context: Kenya has faced persistent corruption and political patronage, particularly since the 2007-2008 post-election violence, which exposed deep ethnic divisions and power struggles. The 2010 Constitution aimed to curb these issues, yet graft remains rampant, with figures like former President Uhuru Kenyatta and current President William Ruto facing scrutiny. The recent push for accountability, highlighted by the 2022 elections and ongoing anti-corruption campaigns, underscores the urgency of addressing these entrenched problems in Kenyan governance.

articles.title: Regulation and trust are key to housing future
articles.summary: We need regulatory structures that protect deposit-holders while allowing developers the...
articles.context: Kenya's housing sector has faced significant challenges, including a housing deficit of over 2 million units as of 2021, exacerbated by rapid urbanization and economic pressures. The government's Big Four Agenda, launched in 2018, aims to address this deficit through affordable housing initiatives. However, ongoing issues with land ownership disputes and regulatory gaps have hindered progress, making the establishment of robust regulatory frameworks essential for restoring investor confidence and protecting homebuyers.

articles.title: Why strong systems matter in patient care
articles.summary: When systems are strong, care is consistent.
articles.context: In Kenya, the ongoing healthcare reforms initiated by the 2010 Constitution aim to decentralize health services and improve access. However, challenges persist, particularly in the wake of the COVID-19 pandemic, which strained resources and highlighted disparities in care. The recent 2022 elections have also shifted political dynamics, with new leadership under President William Ruto focusing on enhancing healthcare systems to ensure consistent patient care across the nation.

articles.title: Shed light on projects
articles.summary: The government should stop its preoccupation with empty public relations gimmicks and ensure...
articles.context: In recent years, Kenya has faced increasing public discontent over government transparency and accountability, particularly following the 2020 Building Bridges Initiative, which aimed to address political divisions but was ultimately declared unconstitutional in 2021. The current administration, under President William Ruto, is under pressure to prioritize tangible development projects over superficial public relations efforts, especially as the country grapples with economic challenges and rising unemployment rates.

articles.title: Issue adequate funds to realise education dream
articles.summary: There is a need for adequate funding to ensure that the dream of accessing quality education is...
articles.context: In Kenya, the 2018 Education Sector Policy aimed to enhance access to quality education, yet funding shortfalls persist, exacerbated by the COVID-19 pandemic's impact on the economy. The ongoing debate over the implementation of the Competency-Based Curriculum (CBC) has further strained resources, with stakeholders like the Kenya National Union of Teachers advocating for increased government investment. As the country approaches the 2024 elections, educational funding remains a critical issue for political candidates and citizens alike.

articles.title: Could media be contributing to rising cases of murder, suicide?
articles.summary: The increase in the incidence of suicide and suicide-murder in recent days is putting the media...
articles.context: In recent months, Kenya has witnessed a troubling rise in suicide and murder-suicide cases, coinciding with heightened political tensions following the 2022 general elections. The ongoing fallout from the election, marked by allegations of electoral malpractice and protests led by opposition figures like Raila Odinga, has exacerbated mental health issues among the populace. This context raises questions about the media's role in shaping public discourse and potentially influencing these tragic outcomes.

articles.title: ترامب يستقبل المفاوضين اللبنانيين والإسرائيليين في البيت الأبيض، وعملية إنزال أمريكية على سفينة تنقل نفطاً إيرانياً

articles.context: تعود جذور الصراع بين الولايات المتحدة وإيران إلى الثورة الإسلامية عام 1979، حيث أدت إلى قطع العلاقات الدبلوماسية. تصاعدت التوترات بشكل ملحوظ بعد انسحاب واشنطن من الاتفاق النووي الإيراني في 2018، مما أدى إلى فرض عقوبات اقتصادية صارمة. في الأسابيع الأخيرة، تزايدت الاشتباكات العسكرية في المنطقة، مما يثير القلق بشأن استقرار الشرق الأوسط ويجعل البحث عن حلول دبلوماسية أكثر إلحاحًا.

articles.title: إيران تدعو الإمارات للعودة إلى مبدأ “حسن الجوار”

articles.context: تأتي تصريحات إسماعيل بقائي في وقت حساس، حيث تتصاعد التوترات بين إيران والإمارات بسبب النزاعات الإقليمية، بما في ذلك الصراع في اليمن. في 2019، أُعيدت العلاقات بين الإمارات وإيران بعد سنوات من التوتر، لكن الاعتقالات الأخيرة في الإمارات تُظهر تدهور هذه العلاقات. كما أن الإمارات تسعى لتعزيز تحالفاتها مع القوى الغربية لمواجهة النفوذ الإيراني المتزايد في المنطقة.

articles.title: NEBE Reports 50.5 Million Voters Registered Amid Election Strains
articles.summary: Ethiopia’s National Election Board has announced that more than 50 million citizens have registered to vote in the country’s upcoming national election, as officials reveal budget shortfalls and reported disruptions in several regions. The Board said 50,514,155 voters had been registered, including more than 45.1 million through manual processes and the remainder digitally. The figures […]
articles.context: Ethiopia's upcoming national election, scheduled for June 2024, comes amid ongoing tensions stemming from the Tigray conflict, which erupted in November 2020 and has since led to widespread humanitarian crises and political instability. The National Election Board's announcement of over 50 million registered voters is significant as it highlights both the country's efforts to conduct a democratic process and the challenges posed by budget shortfalls and disruptions in regions like Oromia and Amhara.

articles.title: هل تحول الجيش السوداني من “قوة دفاع” استعمارية إلى “طائفة” حاكمة؟

articles.context: تاريخ الجيش السوداني معقد، حيث تأسس كقوة دفاع استعمارية في القرن التاسع عشر، وتطور ليصبح لاعباً رئيسياً في السياسة السودانية بعد الاستقلال في 1956. منذ انقلاب 1989 الذي قاده عمر البشير، شهدت البلاد سلسلة من الانقلابات العسكرية، مما أدى إلى تفاقم الصراع بين المدنيين والعسكريين. اليوم، تتصاعد التوترات بعد الإطاحة بالبشير في 2019، مما يثير تساؤلات حول دور الجيش في مستقبل السودان السياسي.

articles.context: تأتي هذه القضية في وقت حساس، حيث تشهد الولايات المتحدة توترات متزايدة بشأن قضايا الهجرة والأمن، خاصة بعد أحداث 6 يناير 2021. بالإضافة إلى ذلك، تتزايد المخاوف من تأثير التكنولوجيا الرقمية على الأمن القومي، مما يجعل قضية شميم مافي، المتهمة بالتخطيط لتهريب معلومات حساسة، محط اهتمام كبير. تتزامن هذه الأحداث مع تصاعد القلق في السودان حول تأثيرات النزاعات الداخلية على الاستقرار الإقليمي.

articles.title: أثيوبيا والدعم السريع؟ التدخل غير القانوني، والتدريب ،العناية الواجبة، والكشف عن الجرائم في حرب السودان

articles.context: تتواصل الحرب الأهلية في السودان منذ أبريل 2023 بين الجيش السوداني وقوات الدعم السريع، مما أدى إلى تفاقم الأوضاع الإنسانية. في هذا السياق، تتهم الحكومة السودانية إثيوبيا بدعم قوات الدعم السريع، وهو ما يعكس التوترات التاريخية بين البلدين، خاصة بعد اتفاقية السلام في جوبا عام 2020. هذه الاتهامات قد تؤدي إلى تصعيد النزاع الإقليمي وتزيد من تعقيد العلاقات بين إثيوبيا والسودان.

articles.title: Years apart, same pain: family mourns father and son lost to police bullets
articles.summary: Juma died of a police bullet while still pursuing justice for a son who met the same fate in 2020.
articles.context: The deaths of Juma and his son highlight the ongoing issue of police violence in Kenya, particularly following the 2017 elections, which were marred by widespread protests and allegations of police brutality. Juma's quest for justice for his son, who was killed in 2020 during protests against police violence, underscores the persistent struggle for human rights in the country.

articles.context: South Sudan, the world's youngest nation, has faced ongoing conflict since its independence in 2011, which has severely disrupted agricultural production. This development follows Israel's increasing engagement with African nations, particularly since the 2016 Israel-Africa Summit.

articles.title: Ethiopia’s quiet energy shift: Ethio telecom’s role in building a green future
articles.summary: In the global race toward decarbonization, significant changes are often driven not by traditional energy giants but by agile institutions that blend digital ecosystems with physical infrastructure. In East Africa, a quiet yet substantial energy revolution is taking shape, with Ethio telecom at the forefront. The company is rapidly transforming from a conventional connectivity provider […]
articles.context: Ethiopia has been undergoing a significant political transition since the appointment of Prime Minister Abiy Ahmed in 2018, which has included economic reforms and a push for digital transformation. Amid ongoing tensions in the Tigray region and efforts to stabilize the country, Ethio telecom is leveraging its infrastructure to support renewable energy initiatives, aligning with Ethiopia's 2025 Climate Resilient Green Economy Strategy, which aims to achieve carbon neutrality by 2030.

articles.title: EU resumes direct budgetary support to Ethiopia
articles.summary: The European Union has officially resumed its direct budgetary support to Ethiopia, which had been suspended since 2020. This move is regarded as a significant boost to the country’s current reform agenda and marks the beginning of a new chapter in the 50-year strategic partnership between the two entities. The resumption of support will result […]
articles.context: The EU suspended its budgetary support to Ethiopia in 2020 due to concerns over human rights violations amid the Tigray conflict, which erupted in November 2020. The resumption of aid comes after the signing of a peace agreement in November 2022 between the Ethiopian government and Tigray People's Liberation Front (TPLF), signaling a potential stabilization in the region and a renewed commitment to reforms under Prime Minister Abiy Ahmed's administration.

articles.title: EU Resumes Budgetary Support to Ethiopian Government
articles.summary: The European Union (EU) has announced it has resumed budget support to Ethiopia, more than five years after the bloc cut payments over concerns of human rights violations during the northern Ethiopia war. The EU is starting the budgetary support with a 140 million Euro allocated for priority sectors. Jozeph Sikela, EU commissioner for international […]
articles.context: The EU's decision to resume budgetary support comes after a prolonged suspension initiated in 2017 due to escalating human rights abuses linked to the Tigray conflict, which erupted in November 2020. The recent peace agreement between the Ethiopian government and Tigray People's Liberation Front (TPLF) in November 2022 has shifted the political landscape, prompting the EU to reassess its engagement with Ethiopia amid ongoing efforts to stabilize the region and promote recovery.

articles.title: 'They told me he was dead': Children born near Kenyan army base learn truth about UK soldier dads
articles.summary: A DNA and legal project has identified the fathers of 20 children born near a military base in Kenya.
articles.context: The UK has maintained a military presence in Kenya since the 1960s, primarily for training purposes. Tensions have risen due to allegations of human rights abuses by British soldiers, particularly following the 2012 High Court ruling that allowed claims from Kenyans against the UK government. The recent identification of fathers through DNA testing highlights the complex legacies of these military operations, particularly in light of ongoing discussions about accountability and reparations for affected communities.

articles.title: school bus quotes
articles.context: In Ethiopia, the ongoing Tigray conflict, which erupted in November 2020, has severely disrupted education and transportation systems. As peace efforts, including the November 2022 Pretoria Agreement, aim to stabilize the region, the safe transport of students remains a pressing concern. Recent incidents involving school buses highlight the challenges faced by families and communities in conflict-affected areas, underscoring the need for reliable and secure educational access amidst ongoing tensions.

articles.title: 'The weapons were loud, but there was always music': Sudanese band play on through the war
articles.summary: One of Sudan's most popular bands, Aswat Almadina, recall being in the studio when the war broke out three years ago.
articles.context: Since the outbreak of conflict in Sudan in April 2023, following a power struggle between the Sudanese Armed Forces led by General Abdel Fattah al-Burhan and the Rapid Support Forces commanded by General Mohamed Hamdan Dagalo, the country has descended into chaos. Amidst the violence, cultural expressions like music have become vital for resilience and unity, highlighting the stark contrast between the brutality of war and the enduring spirit of Sudanese artists like Aswat Almadina.

articles.title: Bondholder, Commercial Creditor Deals Remain Out of Reach in Ethiopia Debt Saga
articles.summary: The co-chairs of Ethiopia’s official creditors committee (OCC) report the country has yet to ink restructuring agreements with bondholders and commercial creditors that comply with its comparability of treatment (CoT) requirements. The committee, chaired by China and France, rejected an agreement in principle reached between the Ethiopian government and holders of its one billion dollar […]
articles.context: Ethiopia has been grappling with a severe debt crisis exacerbated by the Tigray conflict, which began in November 2020 and has led to significant economic strain. The Addis Ababa government has sought debt relief under the G20 Common Framework, but ongoing tensions and the lack of a comprehensive agreement with bondholders and commercial creditors hinder progress. The rejection of the preliminary deal in October 2023 underscores the complexities of Ethiopia's financial negotiations amid its political turmoil.

articles.title: TPLF Central Committee Convenes in Axum as Administrative Rift Deepens
articles.summary: A high stakes meeting of the proscribed Tigray People Liberation Front (TPLF) Central Committee in Axum is intensifying fears of a political rupture in Tigray, with mounting indications that the party could move to reject the region’s interim administration and push to restore its pre-war government structure. The meeting, which began on April 15, 2026, […]
articles.context: The TPLF, once the ruling party in Ethiopia, has been embroiled in a brutal conflict with the federal government since November 2020, following the Tigray War. The November 2022 Pretoria Agreement aimed to cease hostilities, but tensions remain high as the interim administration struggles to gain legitimacy. The TPLF's current meeting in Axum signals a potential shift toward rejecting this interim governance, raising concerns about renewed instability in Tigray and broader implications for Ethiopian unity.

articles.title: Education Ministry’s Digital Exam Scheme Triggers Parliamentary Uproar
articles.summary: The Education Ministry has come under intense parliamentary scrutiny over the effectiveness and readiness of its ambitious plan to conduct the upcoming university entrance exam entirely online, set to take place within the next few months. Earlier this year, the Minister of Education announced a landmark shift from paper-based exams to an entirely online modality […]
articles.context: Ethiopia's transition to online university entrance exams comes amid ongoing tensions following the Tigray conflict, which has strained educational resources and infrastructure. The government's push for digitalization, announced in early 2023, aims to modernize the education system but raises concerns about access and equity, particularly in rural areas. With parliamentary elections approaching in 2024, the effectiveness of this initiative has become a focal point for opposition parties and civil society groups.

articles.title: Illegal Fuel Trade Booms as Supply Disruptions Leave Drivers Idle
articles.summary: Drivers are paying exorbitant prices for fuel in a parallel market that has only grown more lucrative in the wake of the war in Iran and associated global supply disruptions. An anonymous source working as a driver for a private company told The Reporter that he encountered extreme fuel prices while operating in the Gambella […]
articles.context: The ongoing conflict in Ethiopia, particularly the Tigray War that began in November 2020, has severely disrupted supply chains and fuel distribution across the country. Additionally, the recent war in Iran has exacerbated global fuel shortages, pushing prices up in Ethiopia's already strained economy. The government's inability to stabilize fuel supplies, coupled with rising demand, has led to a booming illegal fuel trade, significantly impacting drivers and businesses in regions like Gambella.

articles.title: Justice Can’t Wait: Why Dialogue and Accountability Must Go Hand in Hand
articles.summary: Ethiopia’s Ministry of Justice recently confirmed that the country’s transitional justice process will begin only after the conclusion of the ongoing national dialogue, which is scheduled to conclude in October 2026. At first glance, this sequencing may appear logical: dialogue first, justice later. Yet, this approach potentially undermines both processes. Transitional justice and national dialogue […]
articles.context: Ethiopia has been grappling with internal conflicts, particularly in the Tigray region, since the outbreak of the civil war in November 2020, which has resulted in widespread human rights abuses. The government initiated a peace agreement with the Tigray People's Liberation Front (TPLF) in November 2022, but ongoing ethnic tensions and violence continue to threaten stability. The transitional justice process is crucial for addressing these grievances, yet its delay until after the national dialogue raises concerns about accountability.

articles.title: No African Consensus on Transboundary Waters, Security: Ethiopia
articles.summary: AU requests AUDA-NEPAD for technical, financial support for Ethiopia’s COP32 preparations Ethiopia has raised its concerns regarding lack of African consensus on the nexus of climate change and security, particularly pertaining to transboundary waters and security. The AU has disclosed documents compiling the decisions, declarations and resolutions made during the 39th Assembly of the Union […]
articles.context: Ethiopia's concerns about the lack of African consensus on transboundary waters stem from ongoing tensions over the Grand Ethiopian Renaissance Dam (GERD), which has been a point of contention with downstream countries Sudan and Egypt since its construction began in 2011. The AU's involvement, particularly through the 39th Assembly of the Union held in February 2022, highlights the urgent need for collaborative frameworks addressing climate change impacts on regional security and water resources.

articles.title: IMF Warns Iran Crisis could Stifle Sub-Saharan GDP Growth, Slashes Forecast
articles.summary: Fuel, food, fertilizer inflation rise could drive 20mln people into food insecurity A new forecast from the International Monetary Fund (IMF) warns that Sub-Saharan Africa could be hit hard by the global supply chain disruptions brought on by Washington’s war in Iran, with countries dependent on fuel imports especially vulnerable to the repercussions, while oil […]
articles.context: The ongoing conflict in Iran, exacerbated by U.S. sanctions and military actions, has led to significant disruptions in global oil supply chains. Ethiopia, which relies heavily on fuel imports, faces rising inflation in food and fertilizer, threatening food security for millions. The situation is further complicated by Ethiopia's internal conflicts, including the Tigray War, which has already strained its economy and humanitarian resources since it escalated in November 2020.

articles.title: UN relief chief warns South Sudan nearing famine as crisis deepens
articles.summary: 'The world’s youngest nation stands at a dangerous crossroads,' Tom Fletcher tells Security Council
articles.context: South Sudan has faced ongoing conflict since its independence from Sudan in 2011, with civil war erupting in 2013 and lasting until a peace agreement was signed in 2018. Despite the agreement, violence continues, exacerbated by political power struggles among leaders like President Salva Kiir and opposition figure Riek Machar. The country is currently grappling with severe food insecurity, with the UN warning that millions are at risk of famine as humanitarian aid remains critically underfunded.

articles.title: UN official warns of rising violence, mounting humanitarian crisis in South Sudan
articles.summary: 'Security situation remains deeply concerning ...Civilians continue to bear the brunt,' says head of UN Mission in South Sudan
articles.context: South Sudan has faced ongoing violence since its independence from Sudan in 2011, with civil war erupting in 2013 between forces loyal to President Salva Kiir and former Vice President Riek Machar. Despite the 2018 Revitalized Agreement on the Resolution of the Conflict in South Sudan, tensions remain high, exacerbated by economic instability and intercommunal violence, leading to a dire humanitarian crisis affecting millions. The UN Mission in South Sudan continues to report alarming levels of civilian suffering.

articles.title: OIC condemns Israeli move to appoint envoy in Somaliland
articles.summary: Organization of Islamic Cooperation denounces Israel’s reported plan to appoint envoy in Somaliland, calling it a violation of Somalia’s sovereignty
articles.context: The Organization of Islamic Cooperation (OIC) has historically supported Somalia's territorial integrity, particularly in light of Somaliland's self-declared independence in 1991, which remains unrecognized internationally. Tensions have escalated since Somaliland's increasing diplomatic engagements, including a 2020 agreement with Israel for economic cooperation. This recent Israeli move to appoint an envoy could undermine Somalia's claims over Somaliland and exacerbate regional tensions, especially amid ongoing discussions about Somali federalism and sovereignty.

articles.title: Somalia says Israeli move on Somaliland violates sovereignty, risks regional stability
articles.summary: State Minister Ali Mohammad Omar says Mogadishu rejects recognition of breakaway region, warns of security risks in Red Sea corridor
articles.context: Somaliland, which declared independence from Somalia in 1991, has not been internationally recognized, leading to ongoing tensions between Mogadishu and Hargeisa. The recent Israeli diplomatic overtures towards Somaliland, including potential recognition, threaten to exacerbate these tensions, particularly as Somalia's Federal Government seeks to maintain its territorial integrity. The situation is further complicated by the strategic importance of the Red Sea corridor, which is vital for regional trade and security.

articles.title: Somalia condemns Israeli ambassador's appointment to its breakaway region of Somaliland
articles.summary: Mogadishu declares Tel Aviv's move violation of Somalia’s sovereignty, unity, and territorial integrity, urging international bodies to uphold international law, as it clearly contradicts UN Charter, African Union's foundational principles
articles.context: Somaliland declared independence from Somalia in 1991, following the collapse of the Somali central government, but has not received international recognition. The recent appointment of an Israeli ambassador to Somaliland is seen as a direct challenge to Somalia's claims over the region, exacerbating tensions. This move comes amid ongoing discussions about Somaliland's quest for recognition and Somalia's efforts to maintain its territorial integrity, particularly following the African Union's 2021 reaffirmation of support for Somalia's sovereignty.

articles.title: Airstrikes in Somalia kill 54 al-Shabaab terrorists
articles.summary: Operations also targeted supply stockpiles, significantly weakening group’s logistical capacity, mobility, ability to organize attacks, says Defense Ministry
articles.context: The recent airstrikes come amid intensified military operations against al-Shabaab, a militant group that has been waging an insurgency in Somalia since 2006. Following the Somali government's renewed offensive in early 2022, supported by U.S. airstrikes, the Defense Ministry aims to dismantle al-Shabaab's operational capabilities ahead of the 2024 elections. This offensive is crucial for stabilizing the region and restoring government authority, particularly as Somalia grapples with political fragmentation and ongoing humanitarian crises.

articles.title: Israel appoints 1st ambassador to Somalia's breakaway region in controversial move

articles.context: The appointment of Israel's first ambassador to Somaliland follows Tel Aviv's recognition of the region in December 2025, a move that has intensified tensions with the Somali federal government, which views Somaliland's declaration of independence in 1991 as illegitimate. This diplomatic shift occurs amid ongoing efforts by Somaliland to gain international recognition and strengthen ties with foreign nations, while Somalia grapples with internal instability and competing claims over sovereignty.

articles.title: UN chief calls Sudan conflict world’s ‘largest humanitarian crisis’ 3 years on
articles.summary: ‘Nearly 34 million people inside Sudan now need humanitarian assistance,’ Antonio Guterres says
articles.context: The conflict in Sudan escalated dramatically in April 2023, when fighting broke out between the Sudanese Armed Forces and the Rapid Support Forces, leading to widespread violence and displacement. This ongoing turmoil follows years of political instability, including the ousting of former President Omar al-Bashir in 2019 and subsequent power struggles. The humanitarian crisis has deepened, with nearly 34 million people now reliant on aid, highlighting the urgent need for international intervention and support.

articles.title: 5 miners killed in gold mine collapse in eastern Sudan, medics say

articles.context: The collapse of the gold mine in eastern Sudan highlights the ongoing struggles in the region, exacerbated by the power vacuum following the ousting of President Omar al-Bashir in 2019. The Sudanese Armed Forces and the Rapid Support Forces have been engaged in violent clashes, further destabilizing the area and complicating rescue efforts. Additionally, the gold mining sector has become a focal point for both economic opportunity and conflict, drawing attention from various local and international actors.

articles.title: 'Don’t forget Sudan': African Union chair presses for ceasefire push
articles.summary: 'Sudan is passing through disastrous situation,' Mahmoud Ali Youssouf tells Anadolu, adding that lack of media coverage and shifting attention to Mideast conflict is leading to concerns

The situation escalated into violent clashes in April 2023, resulting in thousands of deaths and a humanitarian crisis. The African Union's call for a ceasefire highlights concerns that the ongoing violence is being overshadowed by conflicts in the Middle East, particularly the Israel-Palestine situation.

articles.title: Sudan slams Germany-hosted conference as ‘colonial tutelage approach’
articles.summary: Foreign Ministry says hosting conference without consulting Khartoum reflects ‘disregard’ for Sudan
articles.context: Sudan's criticism of the Germany-hosted conference comes amid ongoing tensions following the 2021 military coup led by General Abdel Fattah al-Burhan, which derailed the transition to civilian rule established after the 2019 ousting of President Omar al-Bashir. The international community, including the African Union and United Nations, has been involved in mediating Sudan's political crisis, making Khartoum's exclusion from discussions particularly contentious and reflective of its fragile sovereignty.

articles.title: Three years of messages at once - a chronicle of Sudan's war pours in as trapped reporter's phone turns on
articles.summary: As the conflict enters its fourth year, journalist Mohamed Suleiman shudders at what has been lost.
articles.context: Since the outbreak of conflict in Sudan in April 2023, following a power struggle between the Sudanese Armed Forces led by General Abdel Fattah al-Burhan and the Rapid Support Forces commanded by General Mohamed Hamdan Dagalo, the country has descended into chaos. The war has resulted in widespread displacement, humanitarian crises, and a breakdown of communication, making the messages from trapped journalist Mohamed Suleiman particularly poignant as they reflect the dire realities faced by civilians.

articles.title: The human cost of the war in Sudan, three years on
articles.summary: The conflict, which erupted in 2023, has left behind a human toll which is "simply staggering", reports the BBC's Barbara Plett Usher.
articles.context: The war in Sudan began in April 2023, following escalating tensions between the Sudanese Armed Forces (SAF) and the Rapid Support Forces (RSF), a paramilitary group. This conflict emerged from a power struggle after the ousting of long-time leader Omar al-Bashir in 2019, and subsequent failed attempts to establish a stable civilian government. The ongoing violence has resulted in widespread displacement and humanitarian crises, exacerbating regional instability in the Horn of Africa.

articles.title: FACTBOX - 3 years of war: The human cost in Sudan
articles.summary: Sudan’s civil war has uprooted nearly 15M people and killed over an estimated 150,000 as the conflict enters its 4th year with over 33M in need of humanitarian aid and 21M facing a deepening food crisis
articles.context: Since the outbreak of conflict in April 2023, Sudan has been embroiled in a violent power struggle between the Sudanese Armed Forces, led by General Abdel Fattah al-Burhan, and the Rapid Support Forces, commanded by General Mohamed Hamdan Dagalo. This civil war has roots in the 2019 ousting of longtime dictator Omar al-Bashir and has exacerbated existing ethnic tensions, leading to widespread displacement and a humanitarian crisis that has drawn international concern.

articles.title: Fire at Sudan’s Darfur displacement camp leaves 350 families homeless
articles.summary: Blaze in South Darfur camp destroys shelters and forces hundreds of families to relocate amid worsening humanitarian conditions
articles.context: The fire at the displacement camp in South Darfur occurs against the backdrop of ongoing violence and instability in Sudan, particularly following the 2019 ousting of President Omar al-Bashir. The region has seen a resurgence of conflict between government forces and various armed groups, exacerbated by the power struggle between the Sudanese Armed Forces and the Rapid Support Forces since April 2023. This incident highlights the dire humanitarian crisis affecting over 2 million displaced individuals in Darfur.

articles.title: Africa harder hit by Iran war than Germany, says Chancellor Merz

articles.context: The ongoing civil war in Sudan, which escalated in April 2023 between the Sudanese Armed Forces and the Rapid Support Forces, has led to widespread violence and displacement, exacerbating food insecurity in the region. With the conflict disrupting agricultural production and trade routes, Chancellor Merz's warning highlights the urgent need for international attention and support, particularly as the African Union and United Nations prepare for a conference aimed at addressing the humanitarian crisis in Sudan.

articles.title: Somali forces kill 27 al-Shabaab terrorists in 'large-scale operation': Defense Ministry
articles.summary: Key members of terrorist group killed, weapons, military equipment seized

This military action is crucial for stabilizing the region and restoring public confidence in the government.

articles.title: Red Cross warns of worsening Sudan crisis as displacement shifts

articles.context: Sudan has been engulfed in conflict since April 2023, following a power struggle between the Sudanese Armed Forces and the Rapid Support Forces, leading to widespread violence and humanitarian crises. Over 5 million people have been displaced, exacerbating an already dire situation stemming from years of political instability and economic hardship. The recent ceasefire agreements, including the Jeddah Declaration in May 2023, have failed to bring lasting peace, leaving millions in urgent need of assistance.

This collaboration reflects Ethiopia's broader strategy to engage international financial institutions to support its recovery and growth.

articles.title: Ethiopia’s Trailblazing Diplomat Konjit Sinegiorgis Dies

articles.context: The launch of the Wingu Cloud Exchange comes amid Ethiopia's ongoing efforts to enhance its digital infrastructure following the 2020-2022 Tigray conflict, which highlighted the need for robust communication networks. articles.summary: الخرطوم 18 مارس 2026 – نفت وزارة التعليم العالي والبحث العلمي في السودان، الأربعاء، مزاعم استيلائها على جزء من الدعم الإضافي الذي خصصته وزارة المالية للجامعات. وتحدث ناشطون في مواقع التواصل الاجتماعي عن استيلاء الوزارة على 40% من الدعم المؤسسي الذي قدمته وزارة المالية للجامعات. وخصصت وزارة المالية دعماً إضافياً للجامعات في إطار موازنة العام الحالي، لتحسين الوضع المالي لأعضاء هيئة التدريس والكوادر المساعدة وتأهيلهم وتدريبهم، على أن يُصرف حسب المعايير التي توصي بها لجنة التمويل ويجيزها المجلس القومي للتعليم العالي والبحث العلمي. وقالت وزارة التعليم العالي، في بيان، إن “الدعم الإضافي ما زال محفوظاً ولن يتم التصرف فيه لحين إكمال قاعدة بيانات...

articles.summary: الطينة 18 مارس 2026 لقي 16 مدنياً تشادياً على الأقل حتفهم، وأصيب آخرون إثر هجوم شنته طائرة مسيّرة يُرجح تبعيتها للدعم السريع، استهدف تجمعاً للمواطنين في بلدة “مبروكة” التابعة لبلدة الطينة في إقليم “وادي فيرا” التشادي، فيما وجه الرئيس التشادي بالرد على أي هجوم قادم من السودان. ويعد هذا الهجوم الرابع من نوعه خلال الأشهر الأخيرة، بعد أن توغلت قوة من الدعم السريع في يناير الماضي داخل الأراضي التشادية واستهدفت معسكراً للجيش، مما أسفر عن مقتل ثمانية جنود. وأعلنت الدعم السريع وقتها أن الهجوم وقع عن طريق الخطأ وقدمت اعتذارها للحكومة التشادية. ويتزامن هجوم اليوم الأربعاء مع المواجهات العسكرية الضارية التي تشهدها “الطينة” هذا الأسبوع، حيث يتصدى...

articles.title: «أدري».. من شريان حياة إلى بوابة مؤصدة: جحيم الحصار يطارد نازحي دارفور
articles.summary: أدري 18 مارس 2026- أصدرت الحكومة التشادية في فبراير الماضي قراراً فاجأ آلاف النازحين السودانيين العالقين على حدودها الشرقية، بإعلانها إغلاق جميع المعابر الحدودية مع السودان “حتى إشعار آخر”. وكان منفذ “أدري”، الذي يعد الشريان الرئيسي الذي يعبره الفارون من جحيم الحرب في دارفور، أول المغلَقين. لم يكن القرار تشادياً خالصاً، بل جاء تتويجاً لأسابيع من التوتر المتصاعد على الحدود، أدت إلى سقوط قتلى في صفوف الجنود التشاديين. وبين ليلة وضحاها، تحول معبر الأمل إلى بوابة مؤصدة، تاركاً مئات العائلات عالقة في العراء، بين جحيم الحرب خلفها وبوابة مغلقة أمامها.

articles.summary: منتدى الإعلام السوداني بورتسودان، مدني، 18 مارس 2026 (سودان تربيون)– لم تكن آمنة محمد تتخيل أن يأتي رمضان يومًا وهي تحاول إقناع أطفالها بأن القليل من الطعام يكفي للإفطار. نزحت آمنة مع أطفالها الثلاثة من منزلهم في الخرطوم قبل ثلاث سنوات، بعد اشتداد القتال بين الجيش وقوات الدعم السريع، لتجد نفسها في أحد مراكز الإيواء بمدينة بورتسودان، حيث تغيرت ملامح الشهر الكريم تمامًا بالنسبة لأطفالها. تقول آمنة بصوت يختلط فيه الحزن بالصبر: “كان أطفالي ينتظرون رمضان كل عام لشراء الفوانيس والملابس الجديدة، لكن هذا العام لا نفكر إلا في توفير الطعام. أحيانًا لا نجد ما يكفي للإفطار.

articles.summary: طويلة 18 مارس 2026 – أعلنت منظمة أنقذوا الأطفال، الأربعاء، عن إيصال أدوية لدعم 80 ألف طفل و57 ألف بالغ لمدة 6 أشهر في طويلة بولاية شمال دارفور غربي السودان. وتحوّلت طويلة، الخاضعة لسيطرة حركة تحرير السودان بقيادة عبد الواحد نور، من بلدات نائية تفتقر إلى الخدمات إلى مأوى لأكثر من 715 ألف نازح، معظمهم فرّوا من الهجمات على الفاشر ومخيمات النزوح المجاورة لها. وقالت المنظمة، في بيان، إنها أوصلت “شحنة من بورتسودان إلى طويلة لدعم أكثر من 80 ألف طفل و57 ألف بالغ في طويلة، حيث إنها كافية للإبقاء على عمل 20 مرفقًا صحيًا وعيادات متنقلة تخدم المجتمعات النازحة لمدة ستة أشهر”. وأشارت إلى أن الشحنة، التي تحمل 30 طنًا من الأدوية وإمدادات التغذية، وصلت...

articles.title: شبكة أطباء السودان تتهم «الدعم السريع »بتصفية مدنيين قرب بارا
articles.summary: بارا 18 مارس 2026 – قالت شبكة أطباء السودان، الأربعاء، إن قوات الدعم السريع اغتالت 12 مدنيا بينهم نساء وأصابت 3 آخرين بذريعة الانتماء للجيش، في منطقة (شريم ميما) شمالي مدينة بارا بولاية شمال كردفان. وتبادل كل من الجيش السوداني والدعم السريع السيطرة على بارا ثاني أكبر مدن ولاية شمال كردفان عدة مرات، قبل أن تسيطر عليها قوات الدعم السريع هذا الأسبوع. وأفادت الشبكة في بيان، إن “الدعم السريع ارتكبت مجزرة جديدة في منطقة شريم ميما شمالي مدينة بارا، حيث صفّت 12 شخصا بينهم 6 نساء، وأصابت 3 آخرين بجروح متفاوتة، بتهمة انتمائهم إلى الجيش السوداني”. وأشارت الشبكة إلى أن استهداف المدنيين العزّل يمثل انتهاكا صارخا للقوانين والأعراف الإنسانية والدولية.

articles.summary: النهود 17 مارس 2026- أقدمت قوات الدعم السريع، الثلاثاء، على إحراق قافلة تجارية تضم شاحنات كبيرة محملة بمحاصيل زراعية بالقرب من مدينة النهود بولاية غرب كردفان، أثناء توجهها إلى الأبيض بولاية شمال كردفان. ووثق عناصر من القوات عبر مقطع فيديو لحظة إضرام النار في الشاحنات التي كانت محملة بكميات كبيرة من المحاصيل الزراعية، بما في ذلك الذرة والفول السوداني. وتُعد النهود وغبيش وودبندة بولاية غرب كردفان من أبرز مناطق إنتاج الفول في السودان، كما تضم النهود واحدة من أكبر بورصات تداول المحاصيل في غرب السودان، غير أنها تعرضت لعمليات نهب واسعة عقب سيطرة الدعم السريع على المنطقة، الأمر الذي أدى إلى اضطراب النشاط التجاري ودفع العديد من التجار إلى مغادرة...

articles.title: السودان : معدل التضخم يواصل التراجع
articles.summary: الخرطوم17 مارس 2026 – كشف جهاز الإحصاء المركزي في السودان، الثلاثاء، عن تراجع معدل التضخم في فبراير 2026 إلى 56% مقارنة بالشهر الذي سبقه. وبدأ معدل التضخم ينخفض تدريجيًا بعد أن بلغ أعلى مستوياته 422% في يوليو 2021، رغم الانخفاض المتوالي في قيمة العملة المحلية مقابل العملات الأخرى وارتفاع أسعار السلع، حيث سجل 60.26% في يناير الماضي. وقال جهاز الإحصاء، في بيان، إن “معدل التغير في المستوى العام للأسعار – التضخم – في شهر فبراير 2026 زاد بنسبة 56.39% عن نظيره في العام السابق”. وأشار إلى أن معدل التضخم في المناطق الحضرية في شهر فبراير بلغ 72.12%، فيما وصل معدله في المناطق الريفية إلى 54.08%.

articles.title: «بنك السودان»: عجز الميزان التجاري في 2025 بلغ 3.8 مليار دولار
articles.summary: الخرطوم، 16 مارس 2026 – كشف بنك السودان المركزي، الاثنين، عن اتساع العجز في الميزان التجاري للسودان خلال العام الماضي إلى 3.8 مليار دولار. وظل السودان يسجل عجزًا في الميزان التجاري كل عام، يتراوح بين 3 إلى 6 مليارات دولار، قبل أن ينخفض في 2024 إلى 1.7 مليار دولار. وقال البنك المركزي، في تقرير الموجز الإحصائي للتجارة الخارجية لعام 2025، إن “أجمالي الصادرات السودانية بلغ نحو 2.64 مليار دولار، مقابل واردات بلغت 6.49 مليار دولار، مما أدى إلى تسجيل عجز تجاري قدره نحو 3.86 مليار دولار”. وأوضح أن قيمة الصادرات البالغة 2.639 مليار دولار جاءت نتيجة هيمنة قطاع المعادن والمنتجات الزراعية والثروة الحيوانية.

articles.title: «الدعم السريع» تسيطر على بارا بشمال كردفان وكرنوي بشمال دارفور
articles.summary: بارا، 16 مارس 2026 – سيطرت قوات الدعم السريع مرة أخرى، الإثنين، على مدينة بارا ثاني أكبر مدن ولاية شمال كردفان وعلى بلدة كرنوي الحدودية مع تشاد بولاية شمال دارفور. وتقع بارا على بعد نحو 45 كيلومتر شرقي الأبيض عاصمة الولاية، بينما تقع كرنوي ضمن آخر المعاقل الإثنية للقوة المشتركة للحركات التي تقاتل إلى جانب الجيش السوداني. ومنذ اندلاع الحرب بين الجيش السوداني وقوات الدعم السريع في أبريل 2023 سيطرت قوات الدعم السريع على مدينة بارا، ومن ثم استعادها الجيش في سبتمبر 2025. لكن قوات الدعم السريع سيطرت على المدينة مجددا في أكتوبر من العام الماضي، ثم استعادها الجيش قبل 10 أيام، قبل أن تعلن قوات الدعم السريع اليوم الإثنين الاستيلاء على بارا.

articles.summary: الخرطوم 15 مارس 2026 – قرر رئيس الوزراء كامل إدريس الغاء وكالة التخطيط الاقتصادي بوزارة المالية في خطوة تهدف إلى جذب المنح والقروض الدولية َوترقية الأداء الاقتصادي. وشهدت وكالة التخطيط الاقتصادي بوزارة المالية السودانية خلال السنوات الماضية حالات متكررة من الهيكلة، الإلغاء، وإعادة الإنشاء كجزء من محاولات تطوير الهياكل الداخلية لضبط الأداء الاقتصادي. وأصدر رئيس الوزراء، كامل إدريس الأحد “قراراً قضى بإلغاء وكالة التخطيط بوزارة المالية ودمج كامل مهامها واختصاصاتها ضمن الأمانة العامة للمجلس القومي للتخطيط الاستراتيجي”. كما نص القرار في شقه الإداري على إعفاء محمد بشار محمد آدم من منصب وكيل التخطيط بوزارة المالية، في خطوة تعكس توجهاً جديداً...

articles.title: السلطات السودانية تعتقل القيادي الإسلامي الناجي عبد الله
articles.summary: الخرطوم 15 مارس 2026 – اعتقلت السلطات في السودان، الأحد، القيادي الإسلامي البارز الناجي عبد الله، وفقاً لما أفاد به أحد قادة المقاومة الشعبية. وظل الناجي عبد الله وهو أحد قيادات المقاومة الشعبية التي تقاتل إلى جانب الجيش في حربه ضد قوات الدعم السريع يجاهر بمواقفه المؤيدة للقضية الفلسطينية وداعماً لحركة المقاومة الإسلامية “حماس”. وأعلن الناجي في الثالث من مارس الجاري، خلال مخاطبته إفطاراً رمضانياً أمام حشد من مجموعات عسكرية محسوبة على التيار الإسلامي، دعمهم لإيران في الحرب التي تقودها ضد إسرائيل والولايات المتحدة الأميركية. وأثارت تلك التصريحات ردود فعل واسعة حيث أعلن القائد العام للجيش عبد الفتاح البرهان تبرؤه من تلك المجموعة، مؤكداً...

articles.title: «كبر» يكذب صحة مقطع فيديو يتحدث عن «البرهان» أثار جدلًا واسعًا
articles.summary: الخرطوم 16 مارس 2026 – نفى رئيس شورى المؤتمر الوطني – المحلول، والي شمال دارفور الأسبق عثمان محمد يوسف كبر، فجر الاثنين، صحة مقطع فيديو أثار جدلًا واسعًا في السودان تحدث خلاله عن رئيس مجلس السيادة وقائد الجيش عبد الفتاح البرهان. ونشرت قناة “اسكاي نيوز عربية” الإماراتية، الأحد، تقريرًا حوى مقطع فيديو يظهر كبر جالسًا على كرسي مريح يتحدث إلى أشخاص قربه في جلسة يبدو أنها غير رسمية، دون أن تُظهر اللقطات هؤلاء الأشخاص الجالسين قربه. وقال كبر، في مقطع الفيديو، إنهم “تحدثوا للبرهان لتسلميه المجلس العسكري لسحق المتظاهرين وتسليم السلطة إلى الإسلاميين، وهو لم يكن له دور، وما كان محسوبًا ضابطًا، حيث كان يجلس مثل الحرس خارج الباب”. وقال كبر، في بيان،...

articles.title: الصين تعلن  الاستعداد لمساعدة السودان في إعادة الإعمار
articles.summary: بورتسودان 15 مارس 2026 – أعلن القائم بأعمال السفارة الصينية في السودان شيوي جيان، الأحد، وضع مقترحات لمساعدة الخرطوم في إعادة الإعمار ضمن سياسات بكين الخارجية. وتعرضت مدن عديدة، بما في ذلك العاصمة الخرطوم، لدمار واسع طال المؤسسات الخدمية مثل المياه والكهرباء والصحة، إضافة إلى البنية التحتية والمصانع، علاوة على تأثير النزاع على الأنشطة الزراعية والثروة الحيوانية. وقال شيوي جيان، في تنوير صحفي بمدينة بورتسودان شرقي البلاد، إن الصين “على استعداد للمساهمة في إعمار ما دمرته الحرب بالسودان”. وأشار إلى أن بكين ضمنت مقترحات بشأن هذه المساهمة في سياساتها الخارجية للفترة المقبلة.
